questions.question: What is the primary aim of the Analysis of Variance (ANOVA) technique in the context of experimental design? Explain the basic principle with the partitioning of sum of squares.
questions.hint: ANOVA aims to determine whether there are significant differences between the means of different treatment groups by partitioning total variation (SST) into between-groups (SSB) and within-groups (SSW) components.

questions.question: State the main difference between a one-way ANOVA design and a two-way ANOVA design.
questions.hint: In a one-way ANOVA, there is only one independent variable (factor) with multiple levels. In a two-way ANOVA, there are two independent variables, each with multiple levels, and interaction effects can also be tested.

questions.question: Define the F-statistic in ANOVA. What does a large F-value indicate? State the decision rule for the F-test.
questions.hint: F = MSB/MSW. A large F-value indicates between-group variance is much larger than within-group variance. If F > F_critical at significance level alpha, reject the null hypothesis.
